evals.prompt: Improve this stale SKILL.md. I want a bounded loop with clear prompts, fixed evals, and a decision about whether to keep or revert changes.
evals.expected_output: The skill activates as a repo-local optimization workflow, requires freezing the evaluation harness before edits, and includes explicit keep-or-revert logging.
assertions: The workflow requires a frozen evaluation harness or loop charter before mutations start.
The workflow changes one meaningful thing at a time and uses keep-or-revert decisions tied to measured results.
The workflow records experiment history in append-only artifacts such as results/changelog files.

evals.prompt: My prompt benchmark is weak and I keep changing the tests while rewriting the skill. Help me optimize the skill anyway.
evals.expected_output: The skill should refuse benchmark drift inside the same run and direct the maintainer to freeze or restart the comparison track.
assertions: The skill warns against changing the evaluator mid-run.
The skill instructs the user to freeze prompts/evals or start a new comparison track.
The skill does not treat ad hoc rewriting without a stable benchmark as acceptable autoresearch.

evals.prompt: I updated the main SKILL.md and now I think the README, setup prompt, and compact variant might be out of sync. What should the loop do?
evals.expected_output: The skill treats docs/setup/discovery updates as secondary supporting work after the main skill change is justified, and syncs them when positioning changed materially.
assertions: The skill explicitly mentions docs/setup/discovery-surface sync when positioning or discoverability changed materially.
The skill keeps the primary mutable artifact focused even when supporting files are allowed.
The skill does not start with broad multi-file churn before proving the main skill change.
